Colorize.what: Colorize is a two-point duotone / gradient-map. It remaps the image's tonal range so the darkest pixels take one target color ("Remap Black To") and the brightest take another ("Remap White To"), interpolating every intermediate luminance between them. It is the classic way to tint footage into a single-hue look (sepia, cyan-orange, monochrome washes) while preserving contrast. In this engine it shares the channel-mixer module because both are per-pixel linear color remaps.

Remap Black To: The color assigned to the darkest (black-point) pixels. Its `Color Space` child selects how the RGB triple is interpreted; Red/Green/Blue are 0-1 floats.
Remap White To: The color assigned to the brightest (white-point) pixels. With Remap Black To it defines the two ends of the tonal gradient every pixel maps onto.
Intensity: Strength of the recolor, 0-1. 0 leaves the image untouched, 1 applies the full black->white remap. Continuous float despite only 0/1 being sampled.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the colorized result over the original, 0-1 continuous. 0 = bypass, 1 = full effect. Frequently keyframed to animate the tint in/out.
Colorize::HDR In Rec. 709: Working-space toggle: treat the input as Rec.709 HDR when computing the remap. Not a creative control.

Hue/Saturation.what: Hue/Saturation (PAEHSVAdjust) converts each pixel to HSV, rotates its hue, scales its saturation and value, then converts back. It is the standard tool for shifting color casts, boosting/killing saturation, or driving animated color cycles. The engine implements it as a branchless in-place RGB<->HSV round-trip matching FCP's shader.
Hue/Saturation.note: RE finding: FCP's `Hue` param is authored in DEGREES internally (fed to the shader as `hue/360` turns); the corpus stores it in RADIANS (0..2pi). `Saturation` is 0-centered (0 = unchanged, -1 = grayscale). `Value` and `Saturation` are both MULTIPLIERS in FCP, not additive offsets.

Hue: Hue rotation around the color wheel. Stored 0..2pi (pi = 180 deg). 0 = no shift. Rotates every pixel's hue by this angle.
Saturation: Saturation adjustment, 0-centered: 0 = unchanged, -1 = fully desaturated (grayscale), positive = more saturated (up to +3 observed).
Value: Brightness/value multiplier: 1 = unchanged, 0 = black, up to 2 = doubled. Continuous float.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the adjusted result over the original, 0-1 continuous. Often keyframed to animate color shifts.

Gaussian Blur.what: A separable two-pass Gaussian blur. Each output pixel is a Gaussian-weighted average of its neighbors, softening detail with a smooth symmetric falloff. FCP's real implementation decimates the image, convolves a small normalized-Gaussian kernel, then upsamples; the effective screen sigma is approximately radius/6.1. The Horizontal/Vertical percentages let the blur be anisotropic (e.g. a purely vertical smear).

Amount: Blur radius in pixels. 0 = no blur; larger values spread each pixel over a wider Gaussian. The dominant creative knob; often keyframed for focus pulls.
Horizontal: Horizontal blur weighting, 0-100%. 100 = full blur on the X axis, 0 = none. Combined with Vertical to make the blur directional.
Vertical: Vertical blur weighting, 0-100%. 100 = full blur on the Y axis, 0 = none.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the blurred result over the sharp original, 0-1 continuous.

Prism.what: Prism simulates chromatic dispersion: it splits the image into red, green and blue components and offsets them along an axis, as light does passing through a glass prism. The result is colored fringing / rainbow edges scaled by Amount and oriented by Angle. Templates use it for glitchy, refractive, light-bending transition looks.
Prism.note: Not implemented in the TS engine and no checked-in shader; description is from the standard Apple Motion "Prism" filter. The exact per-channel offset geometry (linear vs radial dispersion) is unverified here.

Amount: Magnitude of the chromatic separation. 0 = no split (identity); larger = wider color fringing. Default 7 to pulse the dispersion.
Angle: Direction of the RGB dispersion axis, 0..2pi. Rotates which way the red/blue fringes fan out.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the dispersed result over the original, 0-1 continuous.

Fill.what: Fill replaces every visible pixel's RGB with a single solid Color (or a gradient, unused by the shipping transitions) while preserving the layer's alpha, so it recolors the shape without painting over the transparent surround. Blended by Mix, it is used to flat-color a layer that a downstream keyer or wipe then reveals. Verified faithful to FCP's HgcFillColor shader (recolor + re-premultiply by original alpha).

Fill With: Mode: 0 = solid Color, 1 = Gradient. The shipping transitions always use Color mode.
Color: The solid fill color (nested Red/Green/Blue 0-1 floats) applied to every visible pixel when Fill With = Color.
Gradient: Gradient definition (RGB stops, Opacity, Start/End points, Type) used when Fill With = Gradient. Empty in the corpus templates.
Mix: Blend of the fill toward the original per channel: out.rgb = input + (fill - input) * Mix. 0 = untouched, 1 = fully the fill color. Continuous.

Twirl.what: Twirl applies a swirling rotational distortion around a center point: pixels near the center are rotated most and the rotation falls off with radius, spiraling the image like water down a drain. The `Twirl` angle sets how many radians of spin at the core and `Amount` scales the effect radius/strength. Used for vortex, dizzy, and dissolve-into-a-whirl transition effects.
Twirl.note: Not implemented in the TS engine; description is the standard Apple Motion "Twirl" filter. The exact radial falloff curve is unverified here.

Twirl: Swirl angle at the center in radians (default pi ~= 3.1416, a half-turn). Negative values swirl the opposite direction; the twist decreases toward the edges.
Amount: Scales the strength / falloff radius of the swirl, 0-1. 0 = no distortion. (Corpus mis-sampled the type; this is a continuous float.)
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the twirled result over the original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean despite only 0/1 being sampled.
Center: point2D
Center of the swirl (X,Y) in Motion's normalized frame coordinates, (0.5,0.5) = frame center.

Levels.what: Levels is a tonal remap: it takes input black/white points and gamma and maps them to output black/white points, per channel or on RGB together, exactly like the Levels control in a photo editor. In the corpus the built-in transitions almost always drive only Gamma (via the Histogram > RGB > Gamma sub-parameter) to brighten or darken midtones. FCP internally runs a two-stage affine + power curve (HgcLevels); the TS engine implements the common single-stage form.
Levels.note: RE finding: the Motion UI `Gamma` is fed to the shader as its reciprocal, so a UI Gamma > 1 brightens midtones (net pow(x, 1/gamma)). The input/output black+white points are exposed as children of `Histogram` and are largely unexercised by the shipping transitions.

Histogram: The tonal-mapping controls, nested: Channel selects RGB or a single channel; RGB/Red/Green/Blue hold the black-in, white-in, gamma, black-out, white-out sub-parameters; Opacity blends. Gamma is the dominant driven knob.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the remapped result over the original, 0-1 continuous.
Levels::HDR In Rec. 709: Working-space toggle: interpret input as Rec.709 HDR. Not a creative control.

Brightness.what: Brightness applies a simple per-channel brightness scale/offset to the image (in this engine it maps onto the Levels path; PAEBrightness has no dedicated shader and does an additive/multiplicative lift of RGB with alpha preserved). It is the plain "make it brighter/darker" knob, frequently keyframed to flash or fade a layer.

Brightness: Brightness amount. Default 1 (identity). Values above 1 brighten, below 1 darken; a very wide range (up to ~54) is observed for blown-out flash effects.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the brightened result over the original, 0-1 continuous.

Bump Map.what: Bump Map treats a second image (the Map Image) as a height field and refracts the source through it: brightness gradients in the map become surface slopes that displace the sampled source texcoord, giving the source the illusion of being embossed onto the map's relief. Direction and the Horizontal/Vertical scales control the lighting angle and displacement strength. Verified against the HgcBumpMap shader, which reads the map, builds a per-pixel offset, and resamples the source.
Bump Map.note: HgcBumpMap shader is checked in (evidence/shaders/HgcBumpMap.metal) but the filter is not yet implemented in TS.

Map Image: Reference to the layer used as the height/bump map (stored as a large source-ID integer, not a numeric value). The relief of this image drives the displacement.
Direction: Lighting / displacement direction in radians (default ~0.1745 = 10 deg). Rotates which way the bump slopes push the source samples.
Amount: Displacement strength / bump depth. 0 = flat (no displacement); negative inverts the relief; positive exaggerates it (range -4..10 observed). Heavily keyframed.
Horizontal Scale: Horizontal component of the displacement gain, -10..10. Scales how far map slopes push samples on X.
Vertical Scale: Vertical component of the displacement gain, -10..10. Scales the push on Y.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the bump-mapped result over the original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.
Repeat Edges: When on, sample coordinates that fall outside the frame wrap/repeat rather than clamp.

Gradient Blur.what: Gradient Blur varies blur strength across the frame along a line defined by two points: the image is sharp at one end of the Point 1 -> Point 2 gradient and progressively blurred toward the other, with Amount setting the maximum blur radius. It is the tool for tilt-shift / depth-of-field looks where focus falls off across the frame. FCP's HgcGradientBlur2 shader is checked in.
Gradient Blur.note: HgcGradientBlur2 shader is checked in (evidence/shaders/HgcGradientBlur2.metal) but the filter is not yet implemented in TS.

Point 1: Start of the blur gradient (X,Y, normalized frame coords). Defines the sharp (or fully-blurred) end of the falloff line.
Point 2: End of the blur gradient (X,Y). Blur ramps between Point 1 and Point 2.
Amount: Maximum blur radius reached at the blurred end of the gradient, 0-100. 0 = no blur anywhere.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the gradient-blurred result over the original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Bad Film.what: Bad Film emulates aged/damaged film: it adds dust, hairs, scratches, random per-frame brightness and saturation flicker, focus wobble, and frame jitter to make clean footage look like a scratched print. It is a heavily-parameterized "vintage projector" effect with independent Amount and Variance controls for each artifact type. FCP's HgcBadFilm shader (a brightness/saturation/tint pass over the source) is checked in; the dust/scratch/hair layers are generated separately.
Bad Film.note: HgcBadFilm shader is checked in but the full filter (procedural dust/scratch/hair generation) is not implemented in TS. Corpus dropped 13 localized (non-English) parameter duplicates. Descriptions of the artifact knobs follow the standard Motion "Bad Film" filter.

Focus Amount: Maximum defocus/blur applied by the wobble, 0-64. 0 = always sharp.
Focus Variance: How much the focus randomly varies frame to frame, 0-100.
Brightness Amount: Base brightness multiplier of the aged look, 0.5-5.
Saturate Amount: Base saturation shift, -100..58 (negative desaturates toward a faded print).
Hairs: Number of stray hair/fiber artifacts overlaid, 0-10.
Jitter Amount: Amount of frame position jitter (gate weave), 0-1.
Jitter Variance: How much the jitter magnitude varies, 0-0.5.
Frequency of Change: How often the random artifacts re-roll, 0-100 (higher = more frantic flicker).
Grain: Film-grain noise amount, 0-1. Continuous float, heavily keyframed.
Random Seed: Seed for the artifact RNG; changing it reshuffles the dust/scratch/jitter pattern.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the aged result over the clean original, 0-1 continuous.

Offset.what: Offset scrolls the image by a horizontal and vertical pixel amount, wrapping it around the frame edges (a torus roll). It is the simplest positional filter, used to slide or wrap content for scrolling backgrounds and roll transitions.
Offset.note: Not implemented in the TS engine and no checked-in shader; described as the standard Motion "Offset" scroll/wrap. Whether the shipping filter wraps or clamps at the edge is unverified here (the wide negative observed range suggests large scrolls).

Horizontal Offset: Pixels to shift the image on X. Negative = left, positive = right. Content wraps around. Heavily keyframed for scrolls.
Vertical Offset: Pixels to shift the image on Y. Negative = up, positive = down. Content wraps around.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the offset result over the original, 0-1 continuous.

Disc Warp.what: Disc Warp wraps the image around a disc/tube: pixels within a Radius of the Center are bent onto a circular surface, producing a bulging cylindrical/spherical warp inside the disc and leaving the outside untouched. It is used for lens-like, orb, and tunnel transition looks.
Disc Warp.note: Not implemented in the TS engine and no checked-in shader; described from the standard Motion "Disc Warp". The exact mapping (cylinder vs sphere) is unverified here.

Center: point2D
Center of the disc (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates, (0.5,0.5) = frame center.
Radius: Radius of the warped disc in pixels (default 150). Pixels inside this radius are warped; outside is unaffected.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the warped result over the original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean (only 1 sampled in corpus).

Flop.what: Flop mirrors the layer about its center: horizontally (left<->right), vertically (top<->bottom), or both (a 180 deg point reflection). It is a lossless axis-aligned pixel permutation with no resampling. Implemented and verified faithful to FCP's pure geometric transform (there is no shader).

Flop: Mirror axis: 0 = Horizontal (mirror left/right), 1 = Vertical (mirror top/bottom), 2 = Both (180 deg point reflection).
Mix: Host-level wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. Always 1 in the corpus; the filter itself is a hard mirror.

Bulge Source.what: Bulge (Bulge Source) pushes pixels radially outward from a center point to create a convex, lens-like magnification bump (or, with negative Scale, a concave pinch). Amount sets the pixel radius of the affected region and Scale the strength/sign of the bulge. Used for fisheye pops, magnifier, and elastic-pop transitions.
Bulge Source.note: Not implemented in the TS engine and no checked-in shader; described from the standard Motion "Bulge" filter. The exact radial displacement curve is unverified here.

Amount: Radius of the bulge region in pixels (default 300). Larger = a wider area is distorted.
Center: point2D
Center of the bulge (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Scale: Strength and sign of the bulge, roughly -1.3..4.4. Positive = convex outward bulge, negative = concave pinch, 0 = no distortion.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the bulged result over the original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Color Balance.what: Color Balance shifts the color in three tonal ranges independently -- Shadows, Midtones and Highlights -- letting you warm the shadows and cool the highlights (or any combination) for color grading. Boost controls overall intensity and Clip Color Values sets clamping behavior. It is the classic three-way color-grade control.
Color Balance.note: Not implemented in the TS engine and no checked-in shader; described from the standard Motion "Color Balance". The exact luminance masks that separate shadows/mids/highlights are unverified here.

Shadows: Color shift applied to the darkest tones (nested Red/Green/Blue + Color Space).
Midtones: Color shift applied to the mid tones (nested RGB + Color Space).
Highlights: Color shift applied to the brightest tones (nested RGB + Color Space).
Boost: Overall strength of the three-way balance, 0-1.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the graded result over the original, 0-1 continuous.
ColorBalance::HDR In Rec. 709: Working-space toggle (Rec.709 HDR). Not a creative control.

Directional Blur.what: Directional Blur smears the image along a single axis: FCP rotates the frame so the blur axis is horizontal, applies a 1-D Gaussian, then rotates back. Amount is the blur distance and Angle the direction, giving a motion-streak look. Implemented in TS (the current impl uses a uniform box average rather than FCP's Gaussian falloff -- a documented Phase-2 gap).

Amount: Blur distance in pixels along the axis. 0 = no blur. The primary streak-length knob.
Angle: Direction of the blur streak, 0..2pi (0 = horizontal). Rotates the smear axis.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the streaked result over the sharp original, 0-1 continuous.

Channel Blur.what: Channel Blur blurs the R, G, B and alpha channels independently: you can blur, say, only the red channel while keeping green sharp, producing controllable chromatic softening or selective-channel effects. Amount sets the radius and per-channel toggles select which channels get blurred; Horizontal/Vertical weight the blur axis.
Channel Blur.note: Not implemented in the TS engine and no checked-in shader; described from the standard Motion "Channel Blur" (per-channel Gaussian). Behavior follows Gaussian Blur applied selectively.

Amount: Blur radius in pixels applied to the enabled channels, 0-750. 0 = no blur.

Poke.what: Poke pushes the image outward from a center point as if a finger poked the surface: pixels within the effect are displaced radially, scaled by Scale, over a region set by Radius, sampling the source at the poked coordinate. The HgcPoke shader is a per-pixel inverse radial remap (normalize position, scale by distance, resample). The shader is checked in.
Poke.note: HgcPoke shader is checked in (evidence/shaders/HgcPoke.metal) but the filter is not yet implemented in TS.

Center: point2D
Center of the poke (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates. Often keyframed to drive the poke around the frame.
Scale: Strength of the radial push, 0-1. 0 = no distortion.
Radius: Radius of the affected region in pixels (default 300).
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the poked result over the original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Glint.what: Glint adds anamorphic star/streak highlights to bright areas: it thresholds the highlights, blooms them, and casts radial streaks (like the star filter on a camera lens) with controllable number, size, softness and color fringing. It is the sparkle/lens-flare-star effect used on speculars and light sources. Not implemented and no checked-in shader.
Glint.note: Not implemented in the TS engine and no checked-in shader; described from the standard Motion "Glint" filter. Exact bloom/streak math unverified here.

Exposure: Highlight threshold / exposure that controls which pixels glint (higher = only the brightest streak).
Intensity: Overall strength of the glint overlay, 0-3.2. Continuous float.
Glint Angle: Rotation of the streak pattern in radians.
Glow Amount: Strength of the soft bloom halo added under the streaks, 0-40.
Tint: How much the Tint Color is applied to the glint, 0-1.
Tint Color: Color of the glint tint (nested Red/Green/Blue + Color Space).
Color Fringing: Chromatic dispersion along the streaks, 0-28 (rainbow edges).
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the glint over the original, 0-1 continuous.

Pixellate.what: Pixellate snaps each output pixel to the center of a Scale x Scale-pixel grid cell and nearest-samples the source there, producing a blocky mosaic. Scale is the block size in pixels exactly (verified on both axes) and Center anchors the grid. Implemented and verified against headless FCP via the HgcPixellate shader (a coordinate quantize).

Scale: Mosaic block size in pixels (default 8). 1 = no pixelation; larger = coarser blocks. Verified as an exact block size on both axes.
Center: point2D
Grid origin (X,Y) as a fraction of the frame; shifts where the block boundaries fall.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the mosaic over the original, 0-1 continuous.

Contrast.what: Contrast applies an S-shaped tone curve around a Pivot point: for Contrast < 1 it compresses tones toward the pivot (flatter), for Contrast > 1 it expands them (punchier). FCP builds the curve as a cubic Bezier (a rotation of the y=x line by an angle derived from Contrast), so it is NOT a simple scale-around-mid. Reverse-engineered (CONTRAST_RE.md) but not yet shipped, because an affine approximation is faithful only for Contrast < 1.
Contrast.note: Reverse-engineered in evidence/CONTRAST_RE.md; not implemented (the Bezier LUT must be reproduced exactly rather than fitted). For Contrast < 1 the transfer is nearly affine with a fixed point around code 61-62 (NOT 128); for Contrast > 1 it is a genuine S-curve.

Contrast: Contrast strength, 0.3-3. 1 = identity, <1 flattens (compress toward pivot), >1 punches up (expand). The primary knob.
Pivot: The tonal fixed point the curve rotates around, 0-1 (default 0.5). Pixels at the pivot luminance are unchanged.
Smooth Contrast: Toggle: use the smoother Bezier ease vs a harder curve.
Luminance Only: Toggle: apply the contrast to luminance only (preserve chroma) rather than per-channel RGB.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the contrasted result over the original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean (only 1 sampled).

Earthquake.what: Earthquake shakes and twists the frame with a per-frame pseudo-random rigid transform: a small rotation about an Epicenter plus horizontal/vertical translation, optionally composited across several jittered Layers for a blurred multi-exposure shake. Amplitudes are deterministic functions of Twist/Shake and a seeded RNG. Implemented and RE'd (twist is +/- Twist*0.1 radians, shakes are +/- amount*25 pixels).

Twist: Rotational shake amplitude. Per-frame rotation is +/- Twist*0.1 radians about the epicenter. 0 = no rotation.
Horizontal Shake: Horizontal translation amplitude. Per-frame shift is +/- Shake*25 pixels. Keyframed.
Vertical Shake: Vertical translation amplitude. Per-frame shift is +/- Shake*25 pixels. Keyframed.
Layers: Number of jittered copies blended together, 1-8. >1 blurs the shake into a multi-exposure smear.
Epicenter: Center of the rotational shake (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Random Seed: Seed for the shake RNG; changing it reshuffles the jitter pattern.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the shaken result over the original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Add Noise.what: Add Noise overlays procedural random noise onto the image, blended by Amount and a chosen Blend Mode, optionally monochrome and auto-animated over time. It is the grain/static generator used for film-grain, TV-static and texture effects. FCP's HgcAddNoise shader is a simple per-pixel scale+abs blend of a noise field; the shader is checked in.
Add Noise.note: HgcAddNoise shader is checked in (evidence/shaders/HgcAddNoise.metal) but the filter is not yet implemented in TS.

Amount: Strength of the noise added, 0-4. 0 = no noise; the primary knob.
Type: Noise distribution/type, 0-4 (e.g. uniform vs gaussian variants).
Blend Mode: How the noise composites over the image, 0-17 (add, screen, overlay, etc.).
Monochrome: Toggle: single-channel gray noise vs independent per-channel color noise.
Autoanimate: Toggle: re-roll the noise field every frame (animated static) vs a static pattern.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the noisy result over the original, 0-1 continuous.

MinMax.what: MinMax is a morphological erode/dilate: in Minimum mode it shrinks (erodes) light areas by replacing each pixel with the minimum in a Radius window, in Maximum mode it grows (dilates) them with the maximum. It runs a separable X-then-Y pass over the full 2R+1 window on premultiplied RGBA. Implemented and verified against headless FCP.

Radius: Window half-width in pixels (0-250 nominal). 0 = identity (passthrough). Larger = more aggressive erode/dilate.
Mode: 0 = Minimum (erode: shrink light areas), 1 = Maximum (dilate: grow light areas).
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the morphed result over the original, 0-1 continuous.

Underwater.what: Underwater applies a sinusoidal refraction wobble: the source is resampled through a smoothly space-varying displacement field that is the sum of ten sine waves of decreasing frequency and time-drifting phase -- exactly the look of light refracting through a rippling water surface. Size sets wave scale, Speed the animation rate, Refraction the displacement gain. Implemented and RE'd from the HgcUnderwaterFreqSynth + RefractV2 shaders.

Size: Spatial scale of the wave field (default 2). Larger = broader, gentler ripples; smaller = tighter wobble.
Speed: Animation rate of the wave phases (default 0.5). Only the phase advances over time; 0 = a static frozen ripple.
Refraction: Displacement gain -- how far pixels are pushed by the waves (default 100). 0 = passthrough (identity).
Repeat Edges: When on, samples outside the frame wrap/repeat rather than clamp.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the refracted result over the original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Bad TV.what: Bad TV emulates analog-TV glitch: horizontal roll, wavy random-walk horizontal displacement, static/noise overlay, dark scan lines, chromatic aberration and desaturation. It is the CRT/VHS-breakup effect. Implemented and RE'd verbatim from the HgcBadTV / HgcBadTVNoise Metal shaders.

Roll: Vertical roll/scroll speed of the picture (the classic hold-vertical breakup).
Waviness: Amplitude of the wavy horizontal displacement (random-walk per scanline), 0-200.
Static: Amount of noise/static overlaid, 0-1. When >0 the noise-variant shader (HgcBadTVNoise) runs.
Saturate: Saturation shift, -100..100 (negative bleeds color toward gray).
Color Synch: Chromatic aberration / color-sync error, 0-1 (higher = more RGB split).
Number of Scan Lines: How many dark scan lines are drawn across the frame, 1-1035.
Scan Line Brightness: Brightness floor of the dark scan-line bands, 0-5 (lower = darker lines).
Scan Line Percentage: Duty cycle / thickness of the scan lines, 0-1.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the glitched result over the clean original, 0-1 continuous.

Channel Mixer.what: Channel Mixer recomputes each output channel as a weighted sum of the input R, G, B (and optionally alpha) channels plus an offset -- a full per-channel linear color matrix. It is used for creative channel swaps, custom monochrome mixes, and color-space corrections. Implemented and RE'd from the HgcChannelMixer shader (a 4-wide dot per channel, un-premultiply then re-premultiply).
Channel Mixer.note: RE gap: the '...-Alpha' offset column does not behave as a clean per-channel add in real FCP, but every shipping transition sets those offsets to 0, so the exercised path is exact.

Red Output: Weights that build the output red channel: Red-Red, Red-Green, Red-Blue (channel mix) and Red-Alpha (offset).
Green Output: Weights that build the output green channel (Green-Red/Green/Blue mix + Green-Alpha offset).
Blue Output: Weights that build the output blue channel (Blue-Red/Green/Blue mix + Blue-Alpha offset).
Alpha Output: Weights that build the output alpha channel (only used when Include Alpha is on).
Monochrome: Toggle: set all three RGB rows to the same luma weights for a custom grayscale mix.
Include Alpha: Toggle: also mix the alpha channel.
Allow Mono > 1: Toggle: permit monochrome weights that sum above 1 (brighter gray mix).
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the mixed result over the original, 0-1 continuous.

Light Rays.what: Light Rays casts volumetric god-rays from a Center point: bright areas are streaked radially outward (a zoom-blur of the highlights) and glowed, simulating light shafts bursting from a source. Amount sets ray length, Expansion the spread, Glow the bloom. Not implemented and no checked-in shader; described from the standard Motion "Light Rays".
Light Rays.note: Not implemented in the TS engine and no checked-in shader; described from the standard Motion "Light Rays" filter. Exact radial-streak math unverified here.

Amount: Length/strength of the rays, 9-200. Often keyframed to shoot the rays out.
Expansion: How far the rays spread from the center, 0-2.
Glow: Strength of the bloom/glow on the rays, 0.66-8.
Center: point2D
Origin point of the rays (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the rays over the original, 0-1 continuous.

Tint.what: Tint recolors the image toward a single Color using a hard-light-style two-leg curve about luma 0.5 (NOT a simple luma*color lerp), blended by Intensity. It gives a monochrome/duotone wash keyed to a target color. Implemented (shares the channel-mixer module) and RE'd from the HgcTint shader.

Color: The tint target color (nested Red/Green/Blue 0-1). The image is pushed toward this hue.
Intensity: How strongly the tint is applied, 0-1. 0 = untinted, 1 = full tint.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the tinted result over the original, 0-1 continuous.

Compound Blur.what: Compound Blur uses a second image (the Blur Map) to drive blur strength per pixel: bright areas of the map get more blur, dark areas stay sharp, so an arbitrary mask controls where focus falls. Amount is the max radius, Map Channel selects which channel of the map to read, and Invert/Stretch adjust the mask. It is the general depth-of-field-from-a-matte tool.
Compound Blur.note: Not implemented in the TS engine and no checked-in shader; described from the standard Motion "Compound Blur". Behavior follows a variable Gaussian driven by the map channel.

Amount: Maximum blur radius reached where the map is fully bright, 0-300. 0 = no blur.
Blur Map: Reference to the layer used as the per-pixel blur-strength map (stored as a source-ID integer).
Map Channel: Which channel of the map drives blur, 3-4 (e.g. alpha vs luminance).
Invert Map: Toggle: invert the map so dark areas blur instead of bright.
Stretch Map: Toggle: stretch/fit the map to the frame.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Threshold.what: Threshold posterizes luma into two colors: pixels with luminance above the Threshold become the Light Color, below become the Dark Color, with a Smoothness ramp softening the transition. It is a high-contrast two-tone / cutout effect. Implemented and RE'd verbatim from the HgcThreshold shader (luma dot, offset by Threshold, smoothstep by Smoothness, mix dark->light).

Threshold: Luminance cutoff, 0-1 (default 0.5). Pixels brighter than this go to Light Color, darker to Dark Color.
Smoothness: Width of the soft transition band around the threshold, 0-1. 0 = a hard two-tone edge; larger = a smooth ramp.
Light Color: Color assigned to pixels above the threshold (nested RGB + Color Space).
Dark Color: Color assigned to pixels below the threshold (nested RGB + Color Space).
Correct For Alpha: Toggle: account for the source alpha (un-premultiply) when computing luma.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the two-tone result over the original, 0-1 continuous.

Glow.what: Glow blooms the bright areas: it soft-thresholds the highlights, Gaussian-blurs them by Radius, and screen-composites the blurred glow back over the original. Threshold/Softness set which pixels glow, Opacity the glow gain. Implemented (shares the glow module) and RE'd from the three-pass HgcGlow / HgcGlowCombineFx shaders.

Radius: Blur radius of the glow spread, 7-100. Larger = a softer, wider halo.
Threshold: Brightness threshold, 0-1 (default 0.75). Pixels below this don't glow; the mask ramps up around it.
Softness: Width of the threshold ramp, 0-1 (default 0.2). 0 = a hard glow cutoff, larger = a gradual glow onset.
Opacity: Gain/opacity of the glow overlay, 0-3 (default 1.5). Higher = a brighter bloom.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the glowing result over the original, 0-1 continuous.

Variable Blur.what: Variable Blur blurs the frame by an amount that increases with distance from a Center, between an Inner Radius (sharp) and an Outer Radius (fully blurred) -- a radial tilt-shift / focus-ring. Amount is the maximum blur. Not implemented and no checked-in shader; described from the standard Motion "Variable Blur".
Variable Blur.note: Not implemented in the TS engine and no checked-in shader; described from the standard Motion "Variable Blur" (radial focus falloff).

Amount: Maximum blur radius reached beyond the outer radius, 0-100. 0 = no blur.
Inner Radius: Radius of the fully-sharp central region, 0-665. Inside this, no blur.
Outer Radius: Radius at which blur reaches maximum, 0-1000. Blur ramps between inner and outer.
Center: point2D
Center of the focus ring (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the blurred result over the sharp original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Negative.what: Negative inverts the image colors (out = 1 - in per channel), producing a photographic negative. It is a single-purpose invert with only a blend control. Not implemented and no checked-in shader, but the operation is unambiguous.

Mix: Wet/dry blend of the inverted result over the original, 0-1 continuous. Fractional values give a partial-invert wash.

Soft Focus.what: Soft Focus blends the sharp image with a blurred copy to give a dreamy, glowing softness (a diffusion/glamour look) without fully losing detail. Amount sets the blur radius and Strength how much of the blurred layer is mixed in. Not implemented and no checked-in shader; described from the standard Motion "Soft Focus".
Soft Focus.note: Not implemented in the TS engine and no checked-in shader; described from the standard Motion "Soft Focus" (sharp/blurred diffusion blend).

Amount: Blur radius of the soft layer, 2-330. Larger = a broader, dreamier halo.
Strength: How much of the blurred layer is blended into the sharp image, 0-1. 0 = fully sharp.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the soft-focused result over the original, 0-1 continuous.

Defocus.what: Defocus simulates a camera's out-of-focus lens: instead of a Gaussian, it blurs with a polygonal aperture (bokeh) whose shape has a chosen number of Sides and Rotation, so bright points bloom into hexagons/pentagons. Amount is the defocus radius, Gain boosts highlight bokeh. FCP uses the HgcConvolvePass7tapDefocus shader (checked in); the filter is not yet implemented.
Defocus.note: HgcConvolvePass7tapDefocus shader is checked in but the filter is not implemented in TS.

Amount: Defocus / blur-circle radius, 0-80. 0 = in focus. Heavily keyframed for rack-focus pulls.
Sides: Number of aperture-blade sides shaping the bokeh, 3-8 (3 = triangular bokeh, higher = rounder).
Rotation: Rotation of the aperture polygon, 0..~1.75 radians.
Gain: Highlight bokeh gain, 0.05-4 (default 2). Higher = brighter, more defined bokeh discs.
Aspect Ratio: Aspect stretch of the bokeh (anamorphic bokeh when non-square).
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the defocused result over the sharp original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Luma Keyer.what: Luma Keyer makes pixels transparent based on their luminance: it keeps a band of luma (shadows+mids by default) and keys out the rest via a 4-control-point trapezoid tolerance curve baked into a 256-entry LUT. It is the standard brightness-based matte for luma reveals. Implemented and RE'd from the HgcLumaKeyer LUT shader; the vast majority of its 30 parameters are internal keyer state (matte tools, spill suppression) that the shipping templates leave at defaults.
Luma Keyer.note: Of the 30 exposed parameters, only a handful are creative knobs; the rest (MinGreen/MaxGreen/Spill*/Chroma*, Matte Tools sub-params, KeyerIsInitialized, etc.) are internal keyer engine state persisted in the .motr and are left at their defaults by every corpus user. The measured default curve keeps shadows+mids and keys out highlights.

Luma: The luma key definition (threshold band / graph). Static keyer blob in the corpus -- both shipping users get the default curve (keep shadows+mids, key out highlights).
Invert: Toggle: flip which side of the luma band is kept vs keyed out.
Luma Rolloff: Softness of the key edge, 0-1. Higher = a gentler transparency ramp.
Preserve RGB: Toggle: replace only alpha and pass RGB through unchanged (vs also premultiplying).
Matte Tools: Post-key matte cleanup group: Edge Distance, Levels, Shrink/Expand, Erode, Fill Holes, Soften. Left at defaults in the corpus.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the keyed result over the original, 0-1 continuous.

Color Curves.what: Color Curves adjusts tone via editable per-channel and luma curves (Curve 1/2/3 + Luma), the classic curves grade for shaping shadows, mids and highlights independently. Preserve Luma keeps overall brightness while shifting color. Not implemented; the curve data is stored as opaque graph blobs in the .motr.
Color Curves.note: Not implemented in the TS engine and no checked-in shader; the Curve/Luma parameters are stored as opaque graph blobs (their control points are not decoded here). Described from the standard Motion "Color Curves".

Curve 1: Editable tone curve for the first channel (opaque graph blob).
Curve 2: Editable tone curve for the second channel (opaque graph blob).
Curve 3: Editable tone curve for the third channel (opaque graph blob).
Preserve Luma: Toggle: keep overall luminance constant while the color curves shift chroma.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the graded result over the original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Stroke.what: Stroke draws an outline around the alpha edge of a layer, with a chosen Width, Color (or Gradient), and Position (inside/outside/center of the edge), plus fade controls to feather it. It is the standard edge-outline effect for titles and shapes. Not implemented; described from the standard Motion "Stroke".
Stroke.note: Not implemented in the TS engine and no checked-in shader; described from the standard Motion "Stroke" filter.

Stroke Type: Fill type of the stroke, 0-2 (e.g. color vs gradient vs texture).
Gradient: Stroke gradient (RGB stops, Opacity, Start/End) when Stroke Type is gradient.
Width: Thickness of the outline in pixels, 1-300 (default 10).
Position: Where the stroke sits relative to the alpha edge, 0-2 (inside / center / outside).
Offset: Shifts the stroke in/out from the edge, -12..40.
Threshold: Alpha threshold that defines the edge to stroke, 0-1.
Fade Inside: Feather of the stroke's inner edge, 0-1.
Fade Outside: Feather of the stroke's outer edge, 0-1.
Hide Source: Toggle: show only the stroke, hiding the original layer content.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the stroked result over the original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Extrude.what: Extrude gives a 2D layer a faux-3D extruded depth: it projects the alpha shape back along an Angle by a Distance, shading the extruded sides between Front and Back brightness for a beveled/3D-text look. Not implemented; described from the standard Motion "Extrude".
Extrude.note: Not implemented in the TS engine and no checked-in shader; described from the standard Motion "Extrude" filter.

Angle: Direction the shape is extruded toward, 0..2pi (default pi/4 = 45 deg).
Extrude Style: Style of the extrusion (e.g. solid vs gradient sides).
Gradient: Gradient applied along the extruded depth (RGB, Opacity).
Front Brightness: Brightness at the near end of the extruded sides, 0-1.42.
Back Brightness: Brightness at the far end of the extruded sides, 0-2.
Back Size: Scale of the far face relative to the front, 0.78-1 (perspective taper).
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the extruded result over the original, 0-1 continuous.

Zoom Blur.what: Zoom Blur streaks the image radially from a Center point (a dolly-zoom / speed-warp blur), optionally adding a rotational Swirl. Amount sets the streak length. Implemented in TS (shares the directional-blur module's radial mode).

Amount: Length/strength of the radial streak, 0-50. 0 = no blur. Heavily keyframed for punch-in bursts.
Swirl: Adds a rotational component to the zoom, -0.03..1. 0 = pure radial zoom; positive = spiral streaks.
Center: point2D
Focus point the blur streaks away from (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the streaked result over the sharp original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Unsharp Mask.what: Unsharp Mask sharpens by subtracting a blurred copy from the original and adding the difference back, boosting edge contrast. Radius sets the blur scale (edge size), Amount the sharpening strength, and Threshold suppresses sharpening of low-contrast (noise) areas. Not implemented, but the algorithm is standard and unambiguous.

Radius: Blur radius of the unsharp kernel, 2-32 (default 7). Larger = sharpens broader edges / halos.
Threshold: Minimum local contrast before sharpening applies, 0-0.57. Higher = leaves flat/noisy areas untouched.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the sharpened result over the original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean (only 1 sampled).

Kaleidotile.what: Kaleidotile tiles the frame into a repeating kaleidoscopic pattern: it takes a Width x Height cell of the image, mirrors/rotates it and tiles it across the frame with a rotation Angle, producing a symmetric mandala/mosaic. FCP's HgcKaleidaTile shader exists in the binary. Not implemented; described from the standard Motion "Kaleidotile".
Kaleidotile.note: Not implemented in the TS engine and no checked-in shader (HgcKaleidaTile exists in the binary but is not extracted here). Described from the standard Motion "Kaleidotile".

Width: Width of the source tile cell, 28-4072 (default 64).
Height: Height of the source tile cell, 64-4000 (default 64).
Center: point2D
Center/origin of the kaleidoscope (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the kaleidoscoped result over the original, 0-1 continuous.

Page Curl.what: Page Curl peels the layer back like turning a page: a corner/edge set by Angle and Rotation lifts and curls with a given Radius, revealing a Back Color behind it, animated by Percent from flat to fully turned. It is the classic page-turn transition. Not implemented; described from the standard Motion "Page Curl".
Page Curl.note: Not implemented in the TS engine and no checked-in shader; described from the standard Motion "Page Curl" filter.

Percent: How far the page is turned, 12-100 (default 50). Animated (87 keyframe curves) to drive the turn.
Angle: Direction the curl travels across the page.
Rotation: Orientation of the curl axis / which corner lifts.
Radius: Tightness of the curl roll, 1-100 (default 20). Smaller = a tighter roll.
Animate: Toggle: drive the curl by the built-in animation vs the Percent param directly.
Direction: Toggle: which way the page turns.
Highlight Color: Color of the specular highlight along the curl (nested RGB).
Back Color: Color revealed on the back of the curled page (nested RGB + Opacity).
Shadow: Strength of the shadow cast by the lifted page, 0-100.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean (only 1 sampled).

mCallouts Simple 2.what: This is a third-party callout template (MotionVFX 'mCallouts'), not a built-in FCP image filter -- it is a published effect/generator that draws an animated callout line + title that tracks a point on screen. Its parameters are template rig controls (Track/Title/Image position groups and OSC settings), not image-processing knobs.
mCallouts Simple 2.note: This is a third-party MotionVFX callout template, NOT a built-in Apple filter. It appears in the corpus purely because templates embed it; its parameters are animation-rig groups (position/scale/angle tracks) rather than pixel operations. No image-processing behavior to reverse-engineer.

Track: Rig group positioning/scaling the tracked callout endpoint (Position X/Y, Scale X/Y, Z Angle).
Title: Rig group positioning the title card (Movement, Far/Close, Position X/Y).
Image: Rig group binding the title/track/line elements together.
Mix: Host-level blend, 0-1. Always 1 in the corpus.

Radial Blur.what: Radial Blur applies a rotational blur about a Center point: pixels are smeared along circular arcs, as if the camera spun during exposure. Angle is the arc of the blur sweep. Implemented in TS (shares the directional-blur module's radial mode).

Center: point2D
Center of rotation the blur sweeps around (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Angle: Arc of the rotational blur sweep in radians (default ~0.524 = 30 deg, max ~0.524 observed). Larger = a longer spin streak.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the spun result over the sharp original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Scrape.what: Scrape (FCP UI: 'Smear', PAEScrape) smears the layer along an axis: pixels past a threshold line (set by Amount, oriented by Rotation, anchored at Center) are dragged/streaked in the axis direction, an inverse-map geometric warp with no blur. Implemented and RE'd verbatim from the HgcScrape shader.
Scrape.note: Corpus dropped 2 localized (non-English) parameter duplicates. FCP calls this 'Smear'; the PAE class is PAEScrape.

Center: point2D
Anchor of the smear (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Rotation: Orientation of the smear axis in radians, 0..2pi.
Amount: Smear strength, 0-200 (default 50). Controls the threshold line position -- how much of the layer past the center gets dragged. 0 = no smear.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the smeared result over the original, 0-1 continuous.

Vignette.what: Vignette darkens (and optionally desaturates) the frame toward the corners using a radial smoothstep mask: the center stays clear out to a Size radius, then falls off over a Falloff band to a darkened edge set by Darken. Implemented and verified faithful (32-44 dB) to the HgcVignette shader.

Size: Radius of the clear (unvignetted) center, ~0.48-1.35 (default 0.6). Larger = the clear area reaches farther out.
Darken: How dark the vignetted edge gets, 0-1 (default 0.3). 0 = no darkening, 1 = edge goes to black.
Falloff: Softness of the inner->outer transition band, ~0.21-1 (default 0.5). Larger = a wider, softer gradient.
Saturation: Desaturation toward the edge, -0.27..1 (default 0.3). Continuous float; pulls edge color toward gray.
Blur Amount: Optional blur of the vignetted edge, 0-47.
Center: point2D
Center of the vignette (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the vignetted result over the original, 0-1 continuous.

Fisheye.what: Fisheye applies an anisotropic radial power warp (barrel/fisheye bulge or pinch) about a Center: the sample offset scales with normalized radius raised to a power derived from Amount, normalized per-axis by frame width and height. Radius scales the effect extent. Implemented and verified (34-36 dB) with the exact model from the HgcFisheye shader.

Amount: Strength (and sign) of the fisheye. Internally exponent = (Amount/30 <= 0) ? 1/(1-Amount/30) : (Amount/30 + 1). Amount 0 = identity; positive = barrel bulge, negative = pincushion. Default 15.
Radius: Scales the normalization radius, 0.23-2 (default 1). Larger = a broader, gentler warp.
Center: point2D
Center of the fisheye (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the warped result over the original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Overdrive.what: Overdrive is a stylized bloom/edge-glow that extracts the brightest parts of the image, blurs and colorizes them into an inner and outer glow, and adds them back over the source for a hot, over-exposed neon look. Size controls how far the glow spreads, Intensity how hot it burns, and the two glow colors tint the inner core and outer halo. Rotation slightly rotates the glow kernel for a streaky flare.

Intensity: Strength/brightness of the glow that is added back, ~0-100 (default 10).
Rotation: Rotates the glow kernel, giving a streaky/directional flare. 0-2pi.
Inner Glow: Color of the hot inner core of the glow.
Outer Glow: Color of the softer outer halo.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the glow result over the original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Overdrive.note: Not implemented in the TS engine; description is the standard Apple Motion "Overdrive" glow filter. Exact glow kernel is unverified.

Wave.what: Wave displaces the image along sinusoidal waves: each row (or column, if Vertical) is shifted horizontally by a sine of its position, producing a rippling flag/water wobble. Amplitude sets how far pixels move, Wavelength the distance between wave crests, and Offset scrolls the wave phase over time (animate it for motion).

Amplitude: Peak displacement of the wave in pixels, ~0-470 (default 10).
Wavelength: Distance between wave crests in pixels, ~4-500 (default 100). Smaller = tighter ripples.
Offset: Phase offset that scrolls the wave; animate to make the wave travel. Default 100.
Vertical: If on, waves run vertically (columns shifted) instead of horizontally.
Repeat Edges: Clamp/repeat edge pixels instead of showing transparent gaps where the image is pushed away.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the waved result over the original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Wave.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Wave" distortion filter.

Outer Glow.what: Outer Glow adds a soft colored halo radiating outward from the bright/opaque areas of the image (typically text or a keyed subject). Radius sets how far the halo spreads, Brightness how intense it is, and the Range picks how much of the tonal range is treated as "glowing". Inner and Outer colors tint the near and far parts of the halo.

Inner Color: Tint of the halo close to the source edge.
Outer Color: Tint of the halo at its outer extent.
Radius: How far the glow spreads outward, ~0-300 (default 2).
Range: Portion of the tonal range treated as the glow source, 0-1 (default 0.25).
Horizontal: Horizontal scale of the glow spread, percent (default 100).
Vertical: Vertical scale of the glow spread, percent (default 100).

Outer Glow.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Outer Glow" filter.

Gradient Colorize.what: Gradient Colorize is a gradient-map: it computes each pixel's luminance and looks that value up in a user-defined color gradient, replacing the pixel color while keeping its brightness structure. Offset scrolls the lookup position along the gradient and Repeats tiles the gradient multiple times across the tonal range (with a Repeat Method for how the ends wrap). The verbatim HgcGradientColorize shader confirms: dot(rgb, luma-weights) -> scale/offset -> fract/repeat -> sample the gradient texture -> optional saturation mix.

Gradient: The color gradient the image's luminance is mapped through (color stops + positions).
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the colorized result over the original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean (shader mixes via hg_Params[7]).
Offset: Scrolls the luminance-to-gradient lookup position; animate for a sweeping color shift. ~0-28.
Repeats: How many times the gradient tiles across the tonal range, ~1-20 (default 1).
Repeat Method: How the gradient ends wrap when Repeats>1 (repeat vs mirror).
Map Channel: Which channel drives the lookup (luminance by default).
Saturation: Blend between the mapped color and its desaturated luma, 0-1 (shader hg_Params[4]). NOT a boolean.

Gradient Colorize.note: Shader-only. The verbatim HgcGradientColorize Metal shader is checked in under evidence/shaders/; not yet ported to TS.

Trails.what: Trails is a temporal echo effect: it composites several delayed, fading copies of the moving image on top of the current frame, leaving a motion-blur/ghosting streak behind anything that moves. Duration sets how far back in time the echoes reach, Echoes how many copies, and Decay whether each successive echo fades out.

Duration: Time span the trailing echoes cover, ~0.01-0.9s (default 0.1).
Decay: Whether/how much each successive echo fades toward transparent. Continuous, not a hard boolean.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the trailed result over the original, 0-1 continuous.

Trails.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Trails" temporal filter.

Indent.what: Indent is an emboss/bump-lighting effect: it treats the image (or a supplied height map) as a relief surface and relights it, carving pseudo-3D indented/raised edges with a directional highlight. Depth sets how pronounced the relief is, Light Rotation the light direction, and the Highlight/Brightness/Ambient controls shape the specular and fill lighting.

Brightness: Overall brightness of the relit surface, ~0-1.6 (default 0.5).
Light Rotation: Direction of the relighting light in radians (default pi/4).
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Indent.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Indent" emboss/relight filter. Several Height Map wiring params are internal.

Neon.what: Neon finds the edges in the image and lights them up like glowing neon tubing, adding a bright colored inner line and a softer outer glow around detected contours. Inner/Outer Glow set the two halo sizes, the Brightness enums pick their intensity tiers, and Edge Intensity controls how strongly edges are detected.

Outer Brightness: Intensity tier of the outer halo (1-2).
Outer Glow: Size of the outer glow halo, ~1-200 (default 45).
Inner Brightness: Intensity tier of the inner glowing line (1-2).

Neon.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Neon" edge-glow filter.

Keyer.what: Keyer is FCP's flagship chroma/color keyer: it pulls a matte from a green- or blue-screen automatically (Autokey samples the dominant backing color) and knocks it out, with grouped controls for color selection, spill suppression, matte tools, and light wrap. In this corpus it appears mostly at defaults with an auto-sampled key. It is a full compositing keyer, not a simple image filter.

Color Selection: The sampled backing color(s) and their selection tolerance.
Spill Suppression: Removes green/blue spill from the retained foreground.
Fill Holes: Fills small transparent holes in the matte, 0-10.
Invert: Invert the matte (key the foreground instead of the backing).
Matte Tools: Shrink/erode/feather/levels controls on the pulled matte.
Light Wrap: Wraps background light around the foreground edges for realistic compositing.
Preserve RGB: Keep original RGB values rather than premultiplying by the matte.
Mix: Wet/dry blend of the keyed result, 0-1 continuous.

Keyer.note: Not implemented. Many of this filter's real_params are persisted internal keyer engine state (MinGreen/MaxGreen/GreenChroma/Strength/etc.) rather than user knobs; only the user-facing groups are documented here.

Simple Border.what: Simple Border draws a solid-colored frame around the image. Width sets the border thickness in pixels, Color its color, and Border Placement whether the border sits inside, centered on, or outside the image edge.

Border Placement: Where the border sits relative to the edge: inside / center / outside (0-2).

Simple Border.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Simple Border" filter.

Line Art.what: Line Art converts the image into a stylized pen-and-ink drawing: it detects edges and renders them as ink strokes on a paper-colored background. Threshold and Smoothness control which edges become ink, while the Paper/Ink colors and Paper Opacity set the drawing's look.

Paper Opacity: Opacity of the paper background, 0-1. NOT a boolean.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Line Art.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Line Art" filter.

Circle Blur.what: Circle Blur blurs the image everywhere except inside a sharp circular region centered on Center, i.e. it keeps a circular area in focus and blurs the surroundings (a radial focus/spotlight blur). Amount sets the blur strength and Radius the size of the in-focus circle.

Center: point2D
Center of the in-focus circle (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Amount: Blur strength applied outside the circle, ~0-100 (default 10).
Radius: Radius of the sharp in-focus circle in pixels, ~25-2600 (default 400).
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Circle Blur.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Circle Blur" filter.

Sharpen.what: Sharpen increases apparent detail by subtracting a blurred copy of the image from the original and adding the difference back, boosting local contrast at edges. The verbatim HgcSharpen shader is exactly out = max(color0 + (color0 - blurred) * Amount, 0), where color1 is the pre-blurred input. Amount scales the edge enhancement.

Intensity: Secondary strength/gain on the sharpen, ~1-2 (default 1). Continuous float.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Sharpen.note: Shader-only. The verbatim HgcSharpen Metal shader (unsharp-mask style: color0 + (color0-blur)*Amount, clamped >=0) is checked in under evidence/shaders/; not yet ported to TS.

Refraction.what: Refraction distorts the image as if seen through a bumpy refractive surface, displacing each pixel by the gradient of a height map (by default derived from the image itself). Refraction sets the displacement strength and Softness blurs the height map for smoother, glassier bending.

Softness: Blur applied to the height field before refracting, 0-1 (default 0.25). Continuous float.
Refraction: Displacement strength; how strongly pixels bend, ~0-200 (default 100).
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Refraction.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Refraction" filter. The Height Map / Map Channel params are internal image-input wiring.

Bloom.what: Bloom is a light-bloom filter: it isolates pixels brighter than a threshold, blurs them, and screens the glowing result back over the image so highlights bleed and "bloom". In this engine it is served by the shared glow module. Amount is the blur/spread of the bloom, Threshold the brightness cutoff, and Brightness the intensity of the added glow.

Amount: Blur radius / spread of the bloom, ~10-32 (default 7).
Threshold: Brightness cutoff above which pixels bloom, ~0-94 (default 75).
Horizontal: Horizontal scale of the bloom spread, percent (default 100).
Vertical: Vertical scale of the bloom spread, percent (default 100).

Dazzle.what: Dazzle adds anamorphic star/streak flares to the bright points of the image: thresholded highlights sprout radiating spikes, producing a sparkly "dazzle" or glint. Spike Count sets how many arms each star has, Angle rotates them, Amount their length, and Threshold which pixels are bright enough to flare.

Threshold: Brightness cutoff for which pixels sprout flares, ~24-96 (default 75).
Angle: Rotation of the star spikes, radians (default ~0).
Spike Count: Number of radiating arms per star, 3-10 (default 3).

Dazzle.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Dazzle" star-flare filter.

Stripes.what: Stripes overlays a repeating stripe/line pattern generator over the image. Center positions the pattern, Angle rotates the stripes, and Offset shifts their phase. (Note: this filter is largely a pattern generator; most creative controls live in its OSC.)

Center: point2D
Position of the stripe pattern (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Offset: Phase offset shifting the stripes across the frame.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Stripes.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Stripes" pattern filter. (unverified) exact stripe geometry.

Fun House.what: Fun House applies a carnival funhouse-mirror distortion, stretching and squeezing the image around a vertical (or angled) axis through Center as if reflected in a warped mirror. Width sets the region affected, Amount the distortion strength, and Angle the axis orientation.

Center: point2D
Center of the funhouse distortion (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Angle: Orientation of the distortion axis, radians (default 0).
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Fun House.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Fun House" distortion filter.

Halftone.what: Halftone reproduces the image as a printed halftone screen: continuous tone is converted to a grid of variously-sized dots. Scale sets the dot-grid frequency, Angle the screen angle, Contrast the dot hardness, and Center anchors the grid.

Center: point2D
Anchor point of the halftone grid (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Angle: Screen angle of the dot grid, radians (default pi/4).

Halftone.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Halftone" filter.

Gamma.what: Gamma applies a power-law tone curve to the image: out = in ^ (1/Gamma) per channel, brightening midtones for Gamma>1 and darkening them for Gamma<1 while leaving black and white fixed. A single slider.

Gamma: Gamma exponent; >1 brightens midtones, <1 darkens, ~0.14-10 (default 1 = identity).
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Gamma.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Gamma" filter.

Aura.what: Aura wraps a soft glowing halo around the subject, similar to Outer Glow but with separate inner and outer radii defining a ring of brightness. Brightness sets its intensity; the two radii shape where the aura starts and ends.

Inner Radius: Inner edge of the aura ring, ~0-22 (default 2).
Outer Radius: Outer edge of the aura ring, ~0-22 (default 10).

Aura.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Aura" glow filter.

Sphere.what: Sphere wraps the image onto a 3D sphere, mapping the flat frame around a ball centered at Center with the given Radius, so the picture bulges into a globe with the edges receding around the back.

Radius: Radius of the sphere the image is wrapped onto, ~15-915 (default 400).
Center: point2D
Center of the sphere (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Sphere.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Sphere" distortion filter.

Tile.what: Tile repeats the image in a grid of copies filling the frame. Scale sets how many tiles (how small each copy), Stretch the aspect of each tile, Skew/Angle shear or rotate the tiling, and Center anchors the grid.

Center: point2D
Anchor point of the tiling grid (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Skew: Shears the tile grid. Continuous float (default 0).
Angle: Rotation of the tiling grid, radians (default 0).
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Tile.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Tile" filter.

OpenEXR Tone Map.what: OpenEXR Tone Map compresses high-dynamic-range (HDR/EXR) imagery into displayable range using the classic OpenEXR exposure + knee tone-mapping. Exposure applies an overall stop adjustment, Defog subtracts a fog floor, and Knee Low/High define the shoulder where highlights are rolled off gracefully.

Defog: Subtracts a small fog/veiling-glare floor before mapping. Continuous.
Knee Low: Lower knee point where highlight roll-off begins, ~0.3-3 (default 0).
Knee High: Upper knee point controlling how hard highlights compress, ~3.5-7.5 (default 5).
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

OpenEXR Tone Map.note: Not implemented; description is the standard OpenEXR exposure/knee tone-map (as exposed by Apple Motion).

Crystallize.what: Crystallize breaks the image into randomly-shaped crystal cells (a Voronoi/cellular mosaic), filling each cell with a single averaged color so the picture looks like it is seen through cut glass. Size sets the crystal scale, Speed animates the cells, and Feathering softens cell edges.

Speed: Animation rate of the crystal pattern, 0-2 (default 0.5).
Smooth: Smooth the cell coloring rather than flat-fill.

Crystallize.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Crystallize" filter.

Mirror.what: Mirror reflects the image across a line through Center at the given Angle, replacing everything on one side of the line with a mirrored copy of the other side. Rotating the Angle changes the axis of reflection.

Center: point2D
Point the mirror line passes through (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Angle: Angle of the mirror axis, radians (0-~3pi/2).
Repeat Border Pixels: Clamp/repeat edge pixels rather than leaving gaps at the reflection edge.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Mirror.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Mirror" filter.

Comic.what: Comic stylizes the image into a comic-book look: it posterizes colors into flat regions, inks the edges with black outlines, and optionally recolors ink/fill. Style picks the overall treatment, Ink Edges/Smoothness shape the outlines, and Posterize Levels sets how flat the color regions are.

Fill Color: Base fill color used by some styles.
Ink Fill: Amount of ink fill in dark regions, 0-0.5 (default 0.5).
Affect Alpha: Whether the effect also modifies the alpha channel.

Comic.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Comic" filter.

Hatched Screen.what: Hatched Screen renders the image as a cross-hatched line-screen halftone: tone is represented by the density of hatching lines. Scale sets the hatch frequency, Angle rotates it, Skew/Stretch shear the pattern, and Contrast sets how sharply hatching switches on with darkness.

Center: point2D
Anchor point of the hatch pattern (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Angle: Rotation of the hatching lines, radians (default ~0.5).
Contrast: How sharply hatching appears with tone, 0-0.65 (default 0.5).

Hatched Screen.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Hatched Screen" halftone filter.

Black Hole.what: Black Hole warps the image radially as if pulled toward a gravitational point at Center: it displaces sample positions away from the center as a function of distance so the picture appears sucked inward, composited over the original. Amount is the pull strength (FCP UI: "Pulls an object toward a point"). Fully reverse-engineered: it is a MIP pyramid of radial-warp passes whose level count = max(1, round(log2(Amount/8))).

Amount: Pull strength toward the center, 0-1000 (default 150). Drives the number of warp pyramid levels.
Center: point2D
The gravitational center (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates (default 0.5,0.5).
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Relief.what: Relief is a directional emboss that carves the image into a raised/recessed surface lit from a direction defined by two OSC points (Front and Back). Fuzziness softens the relief and the Height Map/Map Channel select the surface source; the result is a chiselled, stone-carving look.

Front: One end of the relief lighting direction (OSC point).
Back: Other end of the relief lighting direction (OSC point).

Relief.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Relief" emboss filter. Front/Back Size and Height Map are internal wiring.

Insect Eye.what: Insect Eye tiles the image into a honeycomb of hexagonal facets, each showing a small refracted copy of the picture, mimicking a compound insect eye. The verbatim HgcInsectEye shader builds a hexagonal grid (the 1.7321 = sqrt(3) constant is the hex-grid metric) and refracts the sample within each cell. Size sets the facet size and Refraction how much each facet distorts.

Refraction: How strongly each facet bends/magnifies its sample, 0-~2 (default 2).
Border Size: Thickness of the dark border between facets. Continuous float.
Border Color: Color of the inter-facet border (see companion HgcInsectEyeBorder shader).
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Insect Eye.note: Shader-only. The verbatim HgcInsectEye (+ HgcInsectEyeBorder) Metal shaders are checked in under evidence/shaders/; not yet ported to TS.

Polar.what: Polar converts the image between rectangular and polar coordinates: with Polar To Rect off it wraps the frame around Center (columns become concentric rings); with it on, it unwraps a polar image back to a rectangle. Used for tunnel/vortex and unwrap effects.

Center: point2D
Center of the polar mapping (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Polar To Rect: Direction of conversion: off = rect->polar (wrap), on = polar->rect (unwrap).
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Polar.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Polar" coordinate filter.

Noir.what: Noir is a preset film-noir color grade that converts the image to a high-contrast, moody black-and-white (or near-monochrome) look. It is a one-knob stylize preset with only a Mix control exposed.

Mix: Wet/dry blend of the noir grade over the original, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Noir.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Noir" preset look. As a canned grade it exposes no creative parameters beyond Mix. (unverified) exact tone curve.

Gloom.what: Gloom is an inverse-bloom / dark-glow: it spreads the dark regions of the image outward (blurring shadows over highlights) for a murky, gloomy diffusion, the tonal opposite of Bloom. Radius sets the spread and Amount the strength.

Radius: Spread radius of the dark diffusion, ~13-100 (default 10).

Gloom.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Gloom" dark-diffusion filter.

Sepia.what: Sepia tones the image toward a warm brown monochrome for an antique-photo look. Amount blends between the original color and the full sepia tone.

Amount: Strength of the sepia toning, 0-1 (default 1). Continuous float.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Sepia.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Sepia" filter.

Droplet.what: Droplet simulates a water droplet/ripple lens on the image: a circular ring at Center refracts the picture like a bead of water, with Radius/Thickness shaping the droplet's lens ring and Height its bulge. Radius is typically animated to make the droplet expand.

Thickness: Thickness of the droplet's refractive ring, ~30-100 (default 40).
Height: Bulge height of the droplet lens, ~-50..50 (default 30).
Center: point2D
Center of the droplet (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.

Droplet.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Droplet" filter. Radius is animated per-instance (kept internal here).

Glass Block.what: Glass Block tiles the image into a grid of rectangular glass bricks, each refracting/magnifying its portion of the picture like glass-block windows. Tile Size sets the brick size, Scale the magnification within each brick, and Angle rotates the grid.

Center: point2D
Anchor of the brick grid (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Angle: Rotation of the brick grid, radians (default 0).
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Glass Block.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Glass Block" filter.

PAETarget.what: Target (PAETarget) creates a concentric target/tunnel distortion: it remaps the image radially around Center so it wraps into rings, with an Angle offset for spiral twist. The verbatim HgcTarget shader computes distance from center, scales it, adds an angular term, and resamples -- a radial coordinate warp.

Center: point2D
Center of the target rings (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates (shader hg_Params[0]).
Angle: Angular twist/offset of the rings, radians (default pi/8).
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

PAETarget.note: Shader-only. The verbatim HgcTarget Metal shader (radial distance remap around Center) is checked in under evidence/shaders/; not yet ported to TS. Node names are usually "Target".

Ripple.what: Ripple sends concentric circular waves rippling out from Center, displacing pixels radially like a stone dropped in water. Amplitude sets the wave height; animate it (or Center) for an expanding ripple.

Amplitude: Height/strength of the ripple waves (default ~50; corpus samples 0-4). Continuous float.
Center: point2D
Center the ripples emanate from (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Ripple.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Ripple" distortion filter. Amplitude was sampled as int in a tiny corpus (5 files) — treat as continuous.

Sliced Scale.what: Sliced Scale is a 9-slice / nine-patch scaler: it divides the image into a 3x3 grid using two slice guides and scales the center and edge regions independently, so corners stay fixed while edges stretch (like scalable UI panels/frames). Slice guides and the Scale point set the grid and target size.

Scale Method: How the sliced regions are scaled (stretch vs tile), 0-2.
Scale: Target scale (X,Y) applied to the sliced image.
Offset: Positional offset of the scaled result.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Sliced Scale.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Sliced Scale" (nine-patch) filter. Expand/Debug are internal groups.

Highpass.what: Highpass keeps only the high-frequency detail of the image, subtracting a blurred (low-pass) copy from the original and centering the result around mid-gray -- the classic frequency-separation / high-pass-sharpen building block. The verbatim HgcHighPass shader is exactly (color0 - blurred)*Amount + 0.5, clamped. Radius sets the blur radius (the frequency split) and Amount the gain.

Amount: Gain on the high-frequency residual (shader hg_Params[0]), ~0-30 (default 15).
Radius: Blur radius defining the low/high frequency split, ~5-100 (default 10).
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Highpass.note: Shader-only. The verbatim HgcHighPass Metal shader ((in - blur)*Amount + 0.5) is checked in under evidence/shaders/; not yet ported to TS.

Spill Suppression.what: Spill Suppression removes colored spill (usually green or blue backing-screen reflections) from a keyed foreground by pulling the chosen Color out of the image. Level sets how aggressively the spill color is neutralized. It is a keyer companion filter.

Color: The spill color to suppress (typically the backing screen's green/blue).
Level: How strongly the spill color is neutralized, ~0.46 (default 0.46).
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Spill Suppression.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Spill Suppression" filter. Spill Contrast/Tint/Saturation are internal sub-controls.

Perspective Tile.what: Perspective Tile maps the image into a four-corner quad (Top Left/Right, Bottom Left/Right) and then tiles that perspective-warped copy to fill the frame, producing a receding tiled-floor/wall look. The corner points define the perspective and Angle rotates the tiling.

Top Left: Top-left corner of the perspective quad.
Top Right: Top-right corner of the perspective quad.
Bottom Right: Bottom-right corner of the perspective quad.
Bottom Left: Bottom-left corner of the perspective quad.
Center: point2D
Anchor of the tiling (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Perspective Tile.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Perspective Tile" filter.

Matte Magic.what: Matte Magic refines an existing matte/alpha channel: it applies levels, shrink/erode, and feathering to clean up the edges of a key. Shrink/Erode tighten the matte and the Levels group remaps its density. It is a matte-cleanup companion, not a standalone key.

Levels: Black/white/gamma remap applied to the matte density.
Feather: Softens the matte edge. Continuous float (default 0).
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Matte Magic.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Matte Magic" matte-cleanup filter.

Line Screen.what: Line Screen renders the image as a printed line-screen halftone: tone is represented by the thickness of parallel lines. The verbatim HgcLineScreen shader takes the pixel luma (dot with hg_Params[5]), compares it against a repeating triangular line profile (fract of a dotted coordinate), and thresholds with a contrast slope. Angle rotates the lines, Scale sets their frequency, and Skew/Stretch shear them.

Center: point2D
Anchor of the line pattern (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Contrast: Threshold slope / hardness of the lines (shader hg_Params[2]), 0-0.5 (default 0.5).

Line Screen.note: Shader-only. The verbatim HgcLineScreen Metal shader is checked in under evidence/shaders/; not yet ported to TS.

Tiny Planet.what: Tiny Planet reprojects an equirectangular 360 image into a stereographic "little planet" -- the ground curls into a small globe with the sky wrapping around the outside. X/Y/Z Rotation orient the sphere before projection and Field of View sets how much of the sphere is squeezed into frame.

X Rotation: Pitch of the sphere before projection, radians.
Y Rotation: Yaw of the sphere before projection, radians.
Z Rotation: Roll of the sphere before projection, radians.
Field of View: How much of the sphere is squeezed into frame, ~360-720 (default 360).
Rotation Order: Order the X/Y/Z rotations are applied.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Tiny Planet.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion 360 "Tiny Planet" projection.

Echo.what: Echo is a temporal filter that overlays a few evenly-spaced previous frames on top of the current one, each fainter than the last, creating a ghosting/echo trail. Delay sets the spacing between echoes, Number how many, and Decay how quickly they fade.

Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Echo.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Echo" temporal filter.

Color Wheels.what: Color Wheels is FCP's primary color-grading control: independent Master/Shadows/Midtones/Highlights color wheels each set color balance, saturation, and brightness for that tonal range, plus Temperature/Tint white-balance controls. It is a full grade panel rather than a single-purpose image filter.

Master: Overall color balance / saturation / brightness wheel.
Shadows: Color wheel affecting the dark tones.
Midtones: Color wheel affecting the mid tones.
Highlights: Color wheel affecting the bright tones.
Preserve Luma: Keep luminance constant while shifting color.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Color Wheels.note: Not implemented; description is the standard FCP "Color Wheels" grading control. Tint/Hue sub-knobs live inside the wheel groups.

Bleach.what: Bleach is a preset bleach-bypass film look: it desaturates and boosts contrast to mimic skipping the bleach step in film development, giving a gritty, silvery, high-contrast image. A canned look with only Mix exposed.

Mix: Wet/dry blend of the bleach-bypass look, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Bleach.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Bleach" (bleach-bypass) preset. No creative parameters beyond Mix. (unverified) exact curve.

Wavy Screen.what: Wavy Screen combines a wavy sinusoidal displacement with a halftone/screen pattern, giving a rippling patterned-screen look. Amplitude/Wavelength shape the wave, Scale the screen frequency, and Contrast the pattern hardness.
Wavy Screen.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Wavy Screen" filter.

360° Reorient.what: 360 Reorient rotates the viewing sphere of an equirectangular 360 image by three Euler angles -- Tilt (pitch, X), Pan (yaw, Y), and Roll (Z) -- resampling the panorama so the horizon/heading changes. All angles are in radians read straight from the .motr. Identity (0,0,0) is an exact passthrough. Implemented in the TS engine.

Tilt (X): Pitch rotation about the X axis, radians.
Pan (Y): Yaw/heading rotation about the Y axis, radians.
Roll (Z): Roll rotation about the Z axis, radians.
Mix: Blend the reoriented result back toward the input, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Chrome.what: Chrome is a preset that gives the image a shiny, metallic chrome-reflection look via a canned bump/environment treatment. Only Mix is exposed.

Mix: Wet/dry blend of the chrome look, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Chrome.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Chrome" preset. No creative parameters beyond Mix. (unverified) exact reflection model.
