Color Emboss.what: Color Emboss embosses the image along a chosen Direction, keeping color (unlike a plain gray emboss) so edges pick up a raised, tinted relief. Direction sets the emboss light angle and Relief toggles the raised vs recessed appearance.

Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Color Emboss.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Color Emboss" filter.

NoiseDither.what: NoiseDither ("Reduce Banding") adds a small amount of dithering noise to smooth gradients, breaking up visible color banding in low-bit-depth or heavily-graded footage. Noisiness sets the dither strength and Movement whether the noise animates per frame.

Movement: Whether the dither pattern animates each frame (vs static).

NoiseDither.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Reduce Banding" (NoiseDither) filter.

Sixties.what: Sixties is a preset psychedelic 1960s color look (bold saturated color shifts / solarization). A canned stylize with only Mix exposed.

Mix: Wet/dry blend of the sixties look, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Sixties.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Sixties" preset. No creative parameters beyond Mix. (unverified) exact color transform.

Mono.what: Mono converts the image to monochrome (black-and-white / single-channel). A one-knob preset with only Mix exposed.

Mix: Wet/dry blend between the mono and original image, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Mono.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Mono" desaturate preset. (unverified) exact luma weights.

Texture Screen.what: Texture Screen maps an external texture image onto the source through a halftone-style screen, so the picture is rendered as tonal modulation of the supplied texture (a patterned-screen / texture-halftone). Contrast/Threshold shape how tone maps to texture and Center/Angle/Scale position the screen.

Center: point2D
Anchor of the texture screen (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Threshold: Tonal threshold where the texture switches on, ~-2..0 (default 0.5).
Noisiness: Amount of noise mixed into the screen, ~6-20 (default 1).

Texture Screen.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Texture Screen" filter. Map Image is an image-input handle; Angle/Skew/Stretch/Scale are pattern-transform sub-knobs.

Scrub.what: Scrub offsets which frame of the source clip is displayed (a time-scrub), optionally blending between frames. Frame offset picks the temporal shift and Frame Blending smooths between offset frames. It is a time filter, not a pixel filter.

Frame offset: Temporal offset (how many frames to scrub). Continuous.
Frame Blending: Blend between adjacent frames for smoother scrubbing.
Offset from: Reference point the offset is measured from.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Scrub.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Scrub" time filter.

PAEColorReduce.what: Color Reduce (PAEColorReduce) quantizes the image to a small set of Match Colors, snapping every pixel to its nearest match color (or replacing matched colors with Replace With). The verbatim HgcColorReduce shader computes squared distance to up to four match colors, picks the nearest, and blends by a Smoothness/contrast slope -- a nearest-palette posterization.

Replace With: Color that matched pixels are replaced with.
Match Color 1: First palette color to match against.
Match Color 2: Second palette color to match against.
Match Color 3: Third palette color to match against.
Match Color 4: Fourth palette color to match against.
Smoothness: Blend slope between matched colors (shader hg_Params[0]). Continuous float, NOT a boolean.
Reduce To: How many palette colors to reduce to, 0-2.

PAEColorReduce.note: Shader-only. The verbatim HgcColorReduce Metal shader (nearest-of-4-colors quantize) is checked in under evidence/shaders/; not yet ported to TS.

Fade.what: Fade simply fades the image toward transparent/black via its Mix (opacity) control -- effectively an opacity ramp exposed as a filter. Animate Mix for a fade in/out.

Mix: Opacity of the image, 0-1 continuous (0 = fully faded). NOT a boolean.

Fade.note: Not implemented; a trivial opacity/fade filter — Mix is its only control.

Angle OSC.what: Angle OSC drives a kaleidoscope: it takes an angular wedge of the image (Segment Angle) around Center and mirrors it repeatedly around the circle, producing a symmetric kaleidoscopic pattern. Offset Angle rotates the source wedge and Partial Segments allows incomplete final wedges.

Center: point2D
Center of the kaleidoscope (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Segment Angle: Angular width of the mirrored wedge, radians (~0.39-2.53, default pi/8).
Offset Angle: Rotation of the source wedge, radians (default ~0.014).
Partial Segments: Allow an incomplete final wedge rather than only whole segments.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Angle OSC.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion kaleidoscope ("Angle OSC") behavior.

Glass Distortion.what: Glass Distortion displaces the image using a supplied distortion map ("Distort Input"), as if viewed through textured glass. Amount sets the displacement strength and Fit/X-Y Scale fit the map to the frame. The distortion source is an image input.

Fit: Fit the distortion map to the frame.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Glass Distortion.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Glass Distortion" filter. Distort Input is an image-map handle; X/Y Scale and Softness are map-fit/soften sub-knobs.

Posterize.what: Posterize reduces the number of tonal levels per channel, snapping smooth gradients into flat bands for a poster-print look. Levels sets how many steps per channel (fewer = more banding).

Levels: Number of tonal levels per channel (default 5; corpus sampled the internal 255 cap). Fewer = harsher banding.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Posterize.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Posterize" filter.

Slit Tunnel.what: Slit Tunnel projects the image into a receding tunnel using slit-scan: a single strip is extruded toward Center with Perspective controlling the depth foreshortening and Speed the scroll rate, with an optional glowing tunnel edge. Used for hyperspace/tunnel transitions.

Center: point2D
Vanishing point of the tunnel (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Glow Color: Color of the tunnel edge glow.
Glow: Intensity of the tunnel glow (default 0.05). Continuous float, NOT a boolean.

Slit Tunnel.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Slit Tunnel" filter. (unverified) exact slit-scan geometry.

Hue/Saturation Curves.what: Hue/Saturation Curves is FCP's curve-based secondary color tool: it lets you reshape color via curves keyed on hue, saturation, and luma (Hue vs Hue, Hue vs Sat, Hue vs Luma, Luma vs Sat, Sat vs Sat, plus a custom range). It is a full curve panel, not a single-slider filter.

Hue vs. Hue: Shifts hue as a function of hue (rotate specific hues).
Hue vs. Saturation: Adjusts saturation as a function of hue.
Hue vs. Luma: Adjusts luma as a function of hue.
Luma vs. Saturation: Adjusts saturation as a function of luma.
Saturation vs. Saturation: Adjusts saturation as a function of saturation.
Custom vs. Saturation: Adjusts saturation for a custom-picked color range.
Preserve Luma: Keep luminance constant while shifting color.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Hue/Saturation Curves.note: Not implemented; description is the standard FCP "Hue/Saturation Curves" tool.

Edges.what: Edges performs edge detection, outlining the high-contrast boundaries in the image (typically over black) for a wireframe/outline look. Intensity scales how strongly edges are drawn.

Intensity: Strength of the detected edges, ~7-20 (default 1). Continuous float.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Edges.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Edges" edge-detect filter.

Transfer.what: Transfer is a preset color-transfer / cross-process look. It exposes only Mix; the exact grade is a canned transform.

Mix: Wet/dry blend of the transfer look, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Transfer.note: Not implemented; a preset color look with only Mix exposed. (unverified) exact transform.

Custom LUT.what: Custom LUT applies a user-supplied 1D/3D color lookup table (.cube etc.) to the image, remapping colors through the LUT. Convert controls input/output color-space handling around the LUT.

LUT: The color lookup table file applied to the image.
Convert: Input/output color-space conversion applied around the LUT.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Custom LUT.note: Not implemented; description is the standard FCP "Custom LUT" filter.

Starburst.what: Starburst radiates streaks of light outward from Center within a given Radius, creating a burst/explosion of rays from a point. Radius sets the burst size.

Center: point2D
Origin of the starburst (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Starburst.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Starburst" filter. (unverified) exact ray geometry.

WideTime.what: WideTime is a temporal smear/trail filter that stretches the image across a span of time (a wide time window), blending recent frames with a Decay falloff for a long motion-blur streak. Duration sets the time window, Decay the fade, and Amount the strength.

Decay: Falloff of older frames, ~0.8 (default 0.8). Continuous float, NOT a boolean.
Amount: Strength of the smear, ~0.8 (default 0.8). Continuous float, NOT a boolean.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

WideTime.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "WideTime" temporal filter.

Cool.what: Cool is a preset that shifts the image's white balance toward cooler (bluer) tones. A canned look with only Mix exposed.

Mix: Wet/dry blend of the cool grade, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Cool.note: Not implemented; a preset cool color look with only Mix exposed. (unverified) exact transform.

Ring Lens.what: Ring Lens refracts the image through a circular ring lens at Center: a torus-shaped glass ring bends the picture where the ring sits (like looking through a magnifying ring), controlled by Radius, ring Thickness, and Refraction index.

Center: point2D
Center of the ring lens (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Refraction: Refraction strength/index of the ring glass, ~-1.5..2 (default 1.7).
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Ring Lens.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Ring Lens" filter.

FxPlugHanging::FxPlug Name.what: This is not a real filter -- it is a placeholder/orphan record left by an FxPlug plugin that failed to load or resolve its name ("FxPlugHanging::FxPlug Name" is the literal fallback identifier). It carries only a generic Input group and Mix. No image behavior can be attributed to it.

Input: Generic passthrough input group of the unresolved plugin.

FxPlugHanging::FxPlug Name.note: Not a built-in filter. This is a dangling/unresolved third-party FxPlug plugin record (name failed to resolve). No pixel behavior to document.

CorridorKey by LateNite.what: CorridorKey by LateNite is a third-party FxPlug keyer plugin (by LateNite Films / CoreMelt-style vendor), not a built-in Apple filter. It is an AI/analysis-driven matte generator with grouped controls for analysis data, subject points, matte, edge & spill, and temporal stability. Its parameters are the plugin's own rig, not standard Motion image knobs.

Interior Detail: Controls for retaining interior matte detail.

CorridorKey by LateNite.note: Third-party plugin, NOT a built-in Apple filter (CorridorKey by LateNite). Analysis Data / Subject Points are opaque analysis blobs. No pixel behavior to reverse-engineer here.

Gyroflow Toolbox.what: Gyroflow Toolbox is a third-party open-source FxPlug plugin that applies Gyroflow gyroscope-based video stabilization inside FCP, using a linked .gyroflow project. Its parameters are project/import/tools rigs and opaque project data, not built-in Motion image controls.

Import: Import a Gyroflow project / gyro data.
Gyroflow Parameters: Stabilization parameters read from the Gyroflow project.
Tools: Utility tools for the stabilization workflow.

Gyroflow Toolbox.note: Third-party open-source plugin, NOT a built-in Apple filter (Gyroflow Toolbox). Project path/bookmark/data params are opaque project handles. No pixel behavior to document.

Rounded · KF.what: "Rounded · KF" is a third-party published FxPlug/Motion template (the " · KF" suffix marks a keyframe-rig template, a naming convention used by template vendors), not a built-in Apple image filter. It carries only an opaque rig parameter and a host Mix; the corpus records it because a template embeds it. No image-processing behavior to attribute.
Rounded · KF.note: Third-party template effect ("Rounded · KF"), NOT a built-in Apple filter. Its single "?" parameter is an unresolved rig group. No pixel behavior to document.

Magic Move · KF.what: "Magic Move · KF" is a third-party published FxPlug/Motion template (the " · KF" suffix marks a keyframe-rig template, a naming convention used by template vendors), not a built-in Apple image filter. It carries only an opaque rig parameter and a host Mix; the corpus records it because a template embeds it. No image-processing behavior to attribute.
Magic Move · KF.note: Third-party template effect ("Magic Move · KF"), NOT a built-in Apple filter. Its single "?" parameter is an unresolved rig group. No pixel behavior to document.

Canvas · KF.what: "Canvas · KF" is a third-party published FxPlug/Motion template (the " · KF" suffix marks a keyframe-rig template, a naming convention used by template vendors), not a built-in Apple image filter. It carries only an opaque rig parameter and a host Mix; the corpus records it because a template embeds it. No image-processing behavior to attribute.
Canvas · KF.note: Third-party template effect ("Canvas · KF"), NOT a built-in Apple filter. Its single "?" parameter is an unresolved rig group. No pixel behavior to document.

Glow · KF.what: "Glow · KF" is a third-party published FxPlug/Motion template (the " · KF" suffix marks a keyframe-rig template, a naming convention used by template vendors), not a built-in Apple image filter. It carries only an opaque rig parameter and a host Mix; the corpus records it because a template embeds it. No image-processing behavior to attribute.
Glow · KF.note: Third-party template effect ("Glow · KF"), NOT a built-in Apple filter. Its single "?" parameter is an unresolved rig group. No pixel behavior to document.

Lumakey.what: Lumakey (node name "Luma Key") keys out pixels based on their luminance, making dark (or bright) areas transparent. Key Mode selects whether low or high luma is keyed. This corpus record exposes only Key Mode; it is the simple luminance keyer variant.

Key Mode: Whether dark or bright luminance is keyed to transparent.

Lumakey.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion luma-key behavior. This single-instance record exposes only Key Mode.

Tonal.what: Tonal is a preset tonal/contrast color look. It exposes only Mix; the underlying grade is a canned transform.

Mix: Wet/dry blend of the tonal look, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Tonal.note: Not implemented; a preset look with only Mix exposed. (unverified) exact transform.

Process.what: Process is a preset cross-process color look (emulating photographic cross-processing). It exposes only Mix.

Mix: Wet/dry blend of the process look, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Process.note: Not implemented; a preset cross-process look with only Mix exposed. (unverified) exact transform.

New York.what: New York is a preset color-grade look (part of Motion's named-city film-look presets). It exposes only Mix.

Mix: Wet/dry blend of the New York look, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

New York.note: Not implemented; a preset city-look grade with only Mix exposed. (unverified) exact transform.

Source.what: Source is effectively a passthrough/source-tap node -- it returns the source image largely unmodified and exposes only a host Mix. It is used inside templates as a source reference rather than as a creative image filter.
Source.note: Not a creative filter; a passthrough/source reference node exposing only Mix. No pixel operation.

PAECircleScreen.what: Circle Screen (PAECircleScreen) renders the image as a halftone screen made of concentric circles/rings around Center. Scale sets the ring frequency and Contrast the hardness. It is the circular sibling of Line Screen / Halftone.

Center: point2D
Center of the ring screen (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Contrast: Hardness of the ring pattern, default 0.5. Continuous float, NOT a boolean.

PAECircleScreen.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Circle Screen" halftone filter. Single-instance record.

PAECICISharpenLuminance.what: Sharpen Luminance (PAECICISharpenLuminance) is a Core-Image-backed sharpen that boosts detail on the luminance channel only, avoiding color fringing. Sharpness sets the strength. It is a CIFilter wrapper (the "CICI" in the class name).

Sharpness: Luminance sharpen strength, default ~0.4. Continuous float, NOT a boolean.

PAECICISharpenLuminance.note: Not implemented; wraps Core Image's CISharpenLuminance. Absolute Points is a coordinate-mode plumbing flag. Single-instance record.

PAECICIMotionBlur.what: Motion Blur (PAECICIMotionBlur) applies a directional (linear) motion blur along Angle over a given Radius, simulating camera/subject movement. It is a Core-Image CIFilter wrapper. Radius sets the blur length, Angle its direction.

Angle: Direction of the motion blur, radians (default 0).

PAECICIMotionBlur.note: Not implemented; wraps Core Image's CIMotionBlur. Absolute Points is a coordinate-mode plumbing flag. Single-instance record.

Parallelogram Tile.what: Parallelogram Tile tiles the image in a parallelogram-shaped grid: each tile is a parallelogram defined by Acute Angle, repeated to fill the frame, with Angle rotating the whole pattern and Tile Size setting the cell size.

Center: point2D
Anchor of the tiling (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Acute Angle: The acute corner angle of the parallelogram cell, radians (default pi/2 = rectangular).
Angle: Rotation of the whole tiling pattern, radians (default 0).
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Parallelogram Tile.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Parallelogram Tile" filter. Single-instance record.

PAEYUVAdjust.what: YUV Adjust (PAEYUVAdjust) converts the image to YUV and lets you tweak the luma (Y) and the two chroma (U, V) channels independently, then converts back -- a color/brightness adjustment in the YUV space used by video. This corpus record is at defaults so only Mix is sampled.
PAEYUVAdjust.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "YUV Adjust" filter. The Y/U/V channel sliders were left at defaults (not sampled) in the single corpus instance; only Mix appears.

PAEYIQAdjust.what: YIQ Adjust (PAEYIQAdjust) is the NTSC-YIQ analogue of YUV Adjust: it converts to the YIQ color space and lets you tweak luma (Y) and the I/Q chroma axes independently. This corpus record is at defaults so only Mix is sampled.
PAEYIQAdjust.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "YIQ Adjust" filter. The Y/I/Q channel sliders were left at defaults (not sampled) in the single corpus instance; only Mix appears.

Noise Dissolve.what: Noise Dissolve is a transition filter that dissolves the image away through a random noise pattern: as Dissolve Amount rises, pixels drop out in a random (Random Seed) speckle order until the image is gone. Used as a grainy dissolve transition.

Dissolve Amount: Fraction of the image dissolved away, 0-1 (animate to drive the transition).
Random Seed: Seed for the dissolve noise pattern; changing it reshuffles the speckle order.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Noise Dissolve.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Noise Dissolve" transition filter. Single-instance record.

Random Tile.what: Random Tile shatters a circular region (Radius) around Center into randomly-offset tiles, scattering the image into a mosaic of displaced squares. Seed sets the random arrangement and Feathering softens tile edges. Used for shatter/scatter transitions.

Center: point2D
Center of the shattered region (X,Y) in normalized frame coordinates.
Feathering: Softness of the tile edges (default 0.5). Continuous float, NOT a boolean.
Seed: Random seed for the tile scatter arrangement.
Mix: Wet/dry blend, 0-1 continuous. NOT a boolean.

Random Tile.note: Not implemented; description is the standard Apple Motion "Random Tile" scatter filter. Single-instance record.
